title: Do not default to thin linear progress bars
summary: AI-generated progress indicators universally default to a thin (4-8px) linear bar with brand-color fill on a gray track, with smooth transition animation, regardless of the progress context or user need for granularity.
instruction: Choose progress representation based on what the user needs to know: determinate vs indeterminate, step count vs percentage, linear vs circular. Match bar prominence to the operation's importance. Reserve smooth animations for operations where continuous progress is measurable.
rationale: The horizontal progress bar is applied uniformly to all progression metaphors — multi-step forms, uploads, activations — though each has different user needs. The smooth animation adds visual delight but delays perceived completion. Bar prominence never varies with operation importance.
violations: Linear bar 4-8px tall, brand-color fill on light gray track
Smooth width transition animation on every bar regardless of discrete vs continuous progress
Same bar format for upload progress, form completion, and system processing
Indeterminate animated stripes for operations that can't measure progress
Percentage or fraction text only when explicitly requested
controls: Step indicator with numbered stages for multi-step processes
Determinate bar with percentage and estimated time for long operations
Circular progress for standalone operations that need compact feedback
Non-linear progress for indeterminate operations (pulse instead of stripes)
borderlineCases: File upload/download where byte-level progress is measurable and meaningful
contextualExceptions: Multi-wizard forms where step number out of total is the primary question
detectorSignal: A thin horizontal rectangle with a colored fill that changes width over time, on a light gray background track.
matcherProposal: Detect elements with progressbar role or ARIA attributes, narrow height (4-12px), with a colored fill element against a track background.
detectorLimitations: Cannot distinguish between appropriate progress bar use and defaulting
falsePositiveScenarios: Standard progress indicators that are appropriate for the context
mitigationStrategy: Flag as a low-severity suggestion, focusing on cases where progress information could be more helpful.

fixturePlans.violation: Thin 6px linear progress bar with brand blue fill, smooth animation, no percentage, for a multi-step form.
fixturePlans.control: Step indicator with numbered circles, current step highlighted, completed steps with checkmarks.
fixturePlans.borderline: File upload progress bar where the linear bar with percentage is the expected standard.

cleanRoomAttestations: Developed without access to proprietary prompt collections.
overlapNotes: Related to loading-states audit (AUD-014) but addresses progress bars in operational contexts, not initial page loading.
